title: Cleistocalyx brongniartii
intro: Cleistocalyx brongniartii, also known as the Bael tree, is a deciduous tree native to Southeast Asia, characterized by its distinctive, aromatic leaves and edible fruits. The health benefits of Cleistocalyx brongniartii include its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which make it useful in treating various health issues, such as diarrhea, fever, and skin infections. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic compounds, particularly xanthones, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Cleistocalyx brongniartii is commonly used in traditional herbal preparations, including infusions, decoctions, and extracts, often in combination with other herbs to treat digestive issues and skin conditions.
taxonomy: Cleistocalyx brongniartii is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Rutaceae, commonly known as the citrus family. This plant is native to tropical Asia and is also known as the 'Borneo bayur' or 'Malay bayur'. It is a species of evergreen tree that is often found in well-drained soils and is valued for its timber and medicinal properties. The plant is further classified within the genus Cleistocalyx, which is a part of the tribe Zanthoxyleae. Cleistocalyx brongniartii is also known to be a part of the order Sapindales, which includes several other families of flowering plants.
uses: Cleistocalyx brongniartii, commonly known as the Bunga Tanjong or Tanjong tree, has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes. The plant is believed to possess antibacterial, anti-inflammatory, and antifungal properties, making it useful for treating wounds and skin conditions. In addition, the leaves of Cleistocalyx brongniartii are said to have antipyretic and antiseptic properties, which can help reduce fever and prevent infections. The plant is also used to treat digestive issues, such as diarrhea and stomach pain, due to its purported anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects. Further research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of using Cleistocalyx brongniartii for medicinal purposes.
